items.briefing:
A phishing kit called Kali365, flagged by the FBI, is expanding globally and could reach Australian inboxes. It makes fake login pages look convincing to steal credentials. Separately, a campaign called DriveSurge is hijacking legitimate websites to trick visitors into running malicious software through fake browser update prompts — a tactic increasingly common in Australia. Over 116,000 systems running Minecraft have also been infected by malware called WeedHack, so staff or family members gaming at home should be cautious about unofficial game downloads.

**Tip:** If a website suddenly asks you to install a browser update, close the tab immediately — legitimate browsers update automatically, not through pop-ups on random websites.

featured.title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked
featured.quote: AI systems exploited to bypass security, directly threatening high-profile user accounts and corporate systems.
